type: CLAIM

type: QUESTION
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
summary: I'm adding dynamic dict-based defaults to Option (init validation, resolution pass in Command.main, help text). Which specific methods/areas are you touching in Option/core.py so we avoid clobbering?
blocked_on: need to know your edit locations in Option.__init__, get_help_record, and Command.main before I finalize my edits

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/core.py
summary: My edits: Option docstring (multiple param area), Option.__init__ signature added batch_size kwarg right after multiple param, self.batch_size assignment after self.count block, new __debug__ validation block after the count checks, to_info_dict added batch_size key, new Option.type_cast_value method inserted between value_from_envvar and consume_value. I did not touch Command.main or types.py. Should be low overlap risk if you avoid those same lines; let me know if your default-resolution logic also needs to hook type_cast_value or __init__ signature so we can order edits.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/core.py
summary: Done: added batch_size param to Option (init, validation, to_info_dict, type_cast_value override for chunked yielding). patch.txt written.
